reforms.description: The French government plans to provide financial support for training in digital technologies specifically aimed at small businesses to enhance their digital capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing digital skills in small businesses is expected to improve productivity and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to provide financial support for training in digital technologies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it targets a critical area for small businesses, aiming to enhance their competitiveness in a digital economy.
reforms.source_quote: The pandemic has speeded up the transition to a digital economy, but the take-up of digital technologies by small businesses remains low. Provide financial support for training in digital technologies for small businesses.

reforms.description: The law on research planning 2021-2030 provides for an increase in the budget of the National Research Agency, aiming to enhance public funding for research and development activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for research is expected to stimulate innovation and improve productivity in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been enacted to increase the budget of the National Research Agency.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in public R&D funding, which is crucial for enhancing France's research capabilities and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The law on research planning 2021-2030 provides for an increase in the budget of the National Research Agency.

reforms.description: The France Relance recovery plan includes EUR 34 billion dedicated to competitiveness and innovation, which encompasses tax cuts, corporate equity-building measures, and support for digitalisation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment in innovation and competitiveness is expected to enhance productivity and stimulate economic growth through improved business performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been rolled out with significant financial commitments.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a substantial investment in innovation and competitiveness, aiming to transform the economic landscape post-COVID-19.
reforms.source_quote: The France Relance recovery plan commits to EUR 100 billion worth of measures, including EUR 34 billion for competitiveness and innovation.

reforms.description: A new investment plan to 2030 was announced, targeting EUR 30 billion for further investments in sectors such as energy, health, and transport.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The targeted investments are expected to drive innovation and efficiency in critical sectors, contributing to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan was announced in October 2021.
reforms.importance_rationale: This investment plan represents a significant commitment to future-oriented sectors, aiming to enhance France's economic resilience and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: In addition, the authorities announced a new investment plan to 2030 in October 2021, worth EUR 30 billion until 2027.

reforms.description: France has introduced the Prêt participatif Relance, a main instrument to support equity finance, which incentivizes the private sector to mobilize long-term loans backed by public guarantees.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to equity finance, the reform aims to reduce corporate debt risks and support business investment, which can enhance overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The instrument has been introduced to support equity finance.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a new financial instrument represents a significant step in addressing corporate financing challenges, although its effectiveness depends on business uptake.
reforms.source_quote: France has introduced several measures to support equity finance, which should help to reduce the risks of high corporate debt.

reforms.description: The 'Un jeune, une solution' plan was initially launched to provide targeted support for young people under 26, including recruitment assistance and training for future job sectors, with a total budget of EUR 6.5 billion.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The plan aims to enhance the employability of young people, thereby increasing their contribution to the economy and supporting overall productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been rolled out with various support measures for young people.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in human capital aimed at addressing youth unemployment and aligning skills with future job market needs.
reforms.source_quote: The 'Un jeune, une solution' plan initially provided 1.3 million 'solutions' targeted at young people under 26 years of age for a total of EUR 6.5 billion announced in July 2020.

reforms.description: The French recovery plan allocates around EUR 3 billion to professional training, aimed at facilitating workforce transitions and enhancing skills development in response to future labor market needs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in professional training enhances the skill set of the workforce, which is crucial for improving productivity and economic resilience.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan dedicates significant resources to professional training initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This substantial investment in training is critical for adapting the workforce to changing economic conditions and enhancing overall productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The France Relance plan dedicates around EUR 3 billion to professional training in 2021-22.

reforms.description: The French government has established the Innovation and Industry Fund (Fonds pour l’Innovation et l’Industrie, FII) to support radical technological innovation, alongside the creation of an Innovation Council to guide innovation policies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aims to enhance R&D investment and stimulate technological advancements, which are crucial for productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund was created in 2018 to support innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a dedicated fund and council represents a significant structural change in how France supports innovation, aiming to boost R&D and technological development.
reforms.source_quote: Moreover, a specific investment fund for supporting radical technological innovation, the Innovation and Industry Fund (Fonds pour l’Innovation et l’Industrie, FII), was created in 2018.

reforms.description: The law on research planning 2021-2030 aims to increase the annual public research effort to EUR 6 billion, enhancing the funding available for public research and improving links between research and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public research funding is expected to enhance the quality and quantity of research outputs, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The law provides for an annual public research effort of EUR 6 billion.
reforms.importance_rationale: This law represents a significant commitment to enhancing public research funding, which is critical for driving innovation and addressing societal challenges.
reforms.source_quote: The law on research planning 2021-2030 and the fourth Investing for the Future Programme (PIA4, 2021-27) provide for work in this area by raising an annual public research effort of EUR 6 billion.

reforms.description: The recovery and resilience plan includes EUR 10 billion in assistance for digitalisation, focusing on supporting the adoption of digital technologies among businesses, particularly small enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing digital capabilities among businesses is expected to improve productivity and competitiveness, particularly for small firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The recovery and resilience plan foresees EUR 10 billion in assistance for digitalisation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The significant funding allocation indicates a strong commitment to enhancing digital capabilities, which is crucial for improving overall business performance.
reforms.source_quote: The recovery and resilience plan...foresees EUR 10 billion in assistance for digitalisation.

reforms.description: The electronic Single Window is set to replace existing networks of business formalities centres by January 2022, aiming to streamline the process of firm creation and improve regulatory efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying business formalities, the reform is expected to enhance the ease of doing business, thereby fostering entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: An electronic Single Window is set to replace the six current networks of business formalities centres by January 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the business registration process, which is expected to have a broad impact on entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: An electronic Single Window is set to replace the six current networks of business formalities centres that are used for firm creation by January 2022.

reforms.description: The 'France 2030' investment plan, announced in October 2021, allocates EUR 15 billion specifically for the green transition, aiming to enhance sustainable practices across various sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This investment is expected to stimulate economic activity and job creation in green sectors, contributing to long-term sustainability and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The investment plan was publicly announced in October 2021.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan represents a significant commitment to green investment, aiming to transform multiple sectors and drive innovation towards sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: The 'France 2030' investment plan, announced in October 2021, earmarks EUR 15 billion to the green transition.

reforms.description: The French government introduced green loans guaranteed by the State to support small firms investing in cleaner technologies, with amounts ranging from EUR 50,000 to EUR 5,000,000 and terms of up to 10 years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These loans incentivize small firms to adopt cleaner technologies, thereby promoting innovation and environmental sustainability.
reforms.status_evidence: Green loans were introduced in early 2021 as part of the recovery plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform provides targeted financial support to small firms, enhancing their capacity to invest in innovative and sustainable practices.
reforms.source_quote: Green loans guaranteed by the State to small firms wishing to invest in cleaner technologies, infrastructures and processes (between EUR 50,000 and EUR 5,000,000 and up to 10 years), introduced in early 2021.

reforms.description: The 'conversion premium' scheme and the 'ecological bonus' have been enhanced to support the sale of electric and hybrid vehicles, contributing to innovation in the automobile sector and increasing sales significantly in 2020.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages the adoption of electric vehicles, reducing emissions and fostering innovation in the automotive industry.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme was temporarily increased to support the automobile sector during the crisis.
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant enhancement of existing support schemes for electric vehicles, which has led to a substantial increase in sales and supports innovation in the sector.
reforms.source_quote: The 'conversion premium' was temporarily increased to support the automobile sector, severely hit by the crisis.

reforms.description: The recovery plan allocates EUR 100 million for the installation of fast charging stations across service areas in the motorway network, promoting the use of electric vehicles.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates the adoption of electric vehicles by improving charging infrastructure, which is essential for reducing emissions and enhancing mobility.
reforms.status_evidence: The recovery plan devotes EUR 100 million to the installation of fast charging stations.
reforms.importance_rationale: A significant investment in infrastructure that supports the transition to electric vehicles, enhancing the overall mobility ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The recovery plan devotes EUR 100 million to the installation of fast charging stations across service areas in the motorway network.

reforms.description: The 'Climate and Resilience Law' introduces a new status of certified public operators to guide households in energy renovation plans, simplifying access to public support and improving the effectiveness of energy renovations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving guidance and access to public support, the reform enhances the effectiveness of energy renovations, leading to better energy efficiency and potential cost savings.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been passed and is set to take effect in 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how public support for energy renovations is administered, aiming to improve efficiency and effectiveness across the sector.
reforms.source_quote: The 'Climate and Resilience Law' creates a new status of certified public operators, who will offer guidance to households in their energy renovation plans.

reforms.description: The introduction of a 'project-by-project' RGE certification aims to simplify certification procedures for energy renovation professionals, allowing smaller businesses to participate in public support programs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By lowering barriers for smaller businesses to obtain certification, the reform encourages more participation in energy renovation projects, enhancing overall market capacity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The project-by-project RGE certification was introduced in January 2021 as part of the recovery plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly improves access to certification for energy renovation professionals, which can enhance the quality and quantity of renovation works.
reforms.source_quote: The introduction of a 'project-by-project' RGE certification in January 2021, as part of the France Relance recovery plan, is expected to simplify the certification procedures.

reforms.description: The French government announced a new investment plan called 'France 2030', which includes financial support of up to EUR 15 billion for research and development of low-carbon technologies, prioritizing nuclear energy, renewables, and green hydrogen.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This investment is expected to enhance innovation in low-carbon technologies, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth in the green energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced a new investment plan in October 2021.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to funding R&D in low-carbon technologies, which is crucial for achieving France's climate goals and enhancing its energy transition.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced a new investment plan in October 2021, called 'France 2030', with financial support of up to EUR 15 billion towards research and development of low-carbon technologies.

reforms.description: In 2021, France published a national framework for designing and shaping environmental compensation measures to improve their implementation and ensure net ecological benefits are achieved.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the effectiveness of environmental measures, potentially leading to better biodiversity outcomes and sustainable development.
reforms.status_evidence: The national framework for environmental compensation measures was published in 2021.
reforms.importance_rationale: This framework represents a significant step towards improving environmental compensation practices, which are crucial for balancing development and biodiversity conservation.
reforms.source_quote: To improve the implementation of environmental compensation measures and ensure that net ecological benefits are achieved, in 2021 France published a national framework for designing and shaping compensation measures.

reforms.description: The 2021 Law on Finance introduced two fiscal incentive measures to make Real Environmental Obligations (REOs) more attractive, aiming to encourage landowners to engage in environmental protection.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages private sector participation in environmental protection, potentially leading to enhanced biodiversity and sustainable land use.
reforms.status_evidence: The 2021 Law on Finance introduced fiscal incentives for Real Environmental Obligations.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of fiscal incentives for REOs represents a significant policy shift aimed at enhancing environmental protection through private sector engagement.
reforms.source_quote: The 2021 Law on Finance introduces two fiscal incentive measures which complement those brought in by the 2016 Law on Biodiversity.
